title: Moira Deeming gives first TV interview since preselection saga
description: Liberal MP Moira Deeming gives her first TV interview since the preselection saga.  “How I’m feeling is a little battered and bruised, but I feel resolute,” Ms Deeming told Sky News host Peta Credlin.  “I feel like I’m free now to do the job that I always wanted to do.”

title: ‘Like an observer in a bar fight’: Albanese blasted for lack of help in Iran war
description: Former Australian major general Mick Ryan does think Australia has the capabilities to help open the Strait of Hormuz.   “The Australian navy has done these kinds of missions before,” Mr Ryan told Sky News host Steve Price.  “If it was a high priority for the government, I’m sure they could find naval assets to go there.  “The Australian government feels like an observer in a bar fight.”

title: ‘Watched by millions’: Pope Leo XIV to deliver Easter ‘message of peace’
description: EWTN Broadcaster Colm Flynn discusses upcoming Easter ceremonies in Vatican City.  “Pope Leo will be taking to St Peter’s Square in what will be a beautiful ceremony,” Mr Flynn told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio.  “Watched by tens of thousands in the square from all over the world, but also millions watching around the globe on television.  “We’re expecting that he will continue that strong message of peace.”

title: ‘Albanese, wake up’: Sky News host blasts PM for ‘wreaking havoc’ across country
description: Sky News host Danica De Giorgio weighs in on the Australian fuel crisis.   “This fuel crisis is wreaking havoc across the country, more so for our farmers,” Ms De Giorgio said.   “As prices soar and supply dwindles down, pressure is building on the agricultural sector.”

title: ‘We should step up’: Tony Abbott urges Australian leadership to 'help' Trump
description: Former prime minster Tony Abbott says Australian leadership should “call the White House” over Iran.  “I don’t like it when the President is critical of Australia, but he has got a point, we should step up,” Mr Abbott told Sky News host Peta Credlin.  “Our military chiefs did not make contact with the Americans; our political leadership did not make contact with the Americans.  “Certainly, had it been me, you’d call the White House, and you'd say to the President, 'How can we help?'”

title: ‘As weak as could be’: PM’s speech shows he has ‘no concept’ of the ‘mess’ farmers are in
description: WA Pastoralists and Graziers Association President Tony Seabrook calls Prime Minister Anthony Albanese’s address to the nation on Wednesday “pathetic”.   Mr Seabrook called his speech a “real dishwash of a presentation” and “weak as could be”.   “I don’t think the guy has any concept of the mess we are in,” Mr Seabrook told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio.

title: Victorian firefighters sent to Adelaide for training in $1.8m hit as facilities face axe
description: Sky News host Caleb Bond reacts to Victorian firefighters being sent to Adelaide for essential training, as 'burn cell' facilities in Victoria face possible decommissioning.

title: More kids unable to tie shoelaces, causing outrage amongst teachers
description: Sky News host Caleb Bond is shocked some kids do not know how to tie their shoelaces.   “It’s shoelaces, how hard can it be?” Mr Bond said.   “We all knew how to do our shoelaces by the time we were in year six.   “Teachers are going well, heck, we’ve got kids going on excursions or sport or whatever, and they can’t do up their own shoes, so all of a sudden the teachers have to do up their shoes for them.”   This comes after a teacher in Victoria sent a message to parents, urging them to teach their children how to tie their shoelaces if they have not already learned.

title: Trump keeping a ‘close eye on’ US Central Commander Brad Cooper
description: Former Australian major general Mick Ryan reveals who is actually running the Iran war from the US’s side.   “I think this is really being run by the combat commander, who is Admiral Cooper, in charge of central command,” Mr Ryan told Sky News host Steve Price.  “His chain of command runs through the Secretary of Defence, Hegseth, up to the President of the United States.  “I have a sense that the president is probably keeping a very close eye on this.”

title: Trump ‘doesn’t know’ what war he is in as he is unsure when Iran conflict will end
description: Former Australian major general Mick Ryan claims US President Donald Trump “doesn’t understand what kind of war he is in”.   “Trump did not provide a vision today of how this war might end,” Mr Ryan told Sky News host Steve Price.   “He said operations might go for two to three more weeks, but they could easily go for much longer than that.”

title: Albanese’s public address was like a ‘patronising school headmaster’
description: Nationals MP Kevin Hogan says Prime Minister Anthony Albanese’s address yesterday was like a “patronising school headmaster”.   “Universally, it was almost ridiculed. One person rang me straight away, and they felt like they were being spoken to like a patronising school headmaster,” Mr Hogan told Sky News host Steve Price.  “People are rightfully anxious.”

title: Iran has been preparing for war with ‘evil Americans’ for ‘five decades’
description: Former Australian major general Mick Ryan claims Iran has been preparing for this war for a “very long time”.   “They’ve been preparing for this for nearly five decades,” Mr Ryan told Sky News host Steve Price.   “They’ve always known that there would be some kind of ultimate battle with what they call the ‘evil Americans’.   “It appears America has probably destroyed about a third to a half of Iran’s missiles.”

title: Trump fires Attorney General and 'loyal friend' Pam Bondi
description: US President Donald Trump has fired Attorney General Pam Bondi, announcing on Truth Social that she would be transitioning to a new role in the private sector.   Trump praised Bondi as a 'great American patriot' and credited her with overseeing a crackdown on crime.   Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche will step in as acting Attorney General.  The president was reportedly unhappy with her handling of the fallout from the Epstein files and felt the DOJ was not aggressively enough pursuing his political opponents.   Bondi becomes the second Cabinet member fired by Trump, following Kristi Noem's removal from the Department of Homeland Security.

title: Australia deploys SAS troops to the Middle East
description: Australia has reportedly deployed SAS troops to the Middle East.  According to the Daily Telegraph, 90 Special Air Service personnel will be based at the Al Minhad Air Base near Dubai in the event of war in the region escalating further.  It is understood that the troops will not be involved in any US attacks but will instead focus on emergency rescues of diplomats in the region.  Defence Minister Richard Marles was asked on Sky News yesterday if Australia was sending any more military assets to the region.  Anthony Albanese is maintaining his stance that Australia will not aid in offensive combat in the conflict.

title: World leaders warn reopening the Strait of Hormuz ‘unrealistic’ after virtual meeting
description: Foreign Minister Penny Wong was among ministers from around 40 countries who met virtually overnight to discuss reopening the Strait of Hormuz.  The vital shipping lane has been closed to most marine traffic since the outbreak of war in Iran.   The meeting was held after Donald Trump's comments that securing the waterway was for others to resolve.   The UK is leading the response.  Leaders from France, Germany, Canada and the United Arab Emirates were among those who attended the meeting.   But France's President Emmanuel Macron told reporters in South Korea it was “unrealistic” to open the Strait by force.
